name: Cuenca, Ecuador

visa.notes: Retirement visa requires $1,375/mo income (3x Ecuador minimum wage). Includes discounts on utilities, flights, and entertainment.

healthcare.notes: IESS public system affordable for residents with pensionado/visa, ~$70-90/person/mo. Private international plans ~$80-180/mo. Anti-VEGF injections available in Quito (Hospital Metropolitano), Cuenca, and Guayaquil; ~$1,200-2,000/injection at private clinics. GLP-1 limited availability, ~$300/mo private. Cuenca is a regional medical hub for retirees with strong specialist density.

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: IESS public system; good private hospitals (Hospital Monte Sinaí). Affordable private care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at private; moderate at IESS public
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Affordable; many meds available without prescription

pros.text: UNESCO World Heritage colonial city (Historic Centre of Santa Ana de los Ríos de Cuenca, listed 1999)

sources.title: UNESCO World Heritage List — Historic Centre of Santa Ana de los Ríos de Cuenca (863)

pros: Very affordable with USD currency (no exchange risk)

cons.text: Altitude (8,400 ft) may cause adjustment issues
Cool climate may not suit warm-weather seekers
Political instability and occasional unrest in Ecuador
Safety & crime: see cited reports for current figures by city and country

taxes.notes: Ecuador uses USD. Income tax on worldwide income if fiscal resident, but retiree visa holders may qualify for exemptions. Progressive rates 0-37%. Jubilado discount benefits offset many costs.
